# v1.19.0 trusted-allowlist FP safety-net (Tier 6).
#
# Real-world weak-key URLs against organisation-controlled hosts that our
# existing imageOnlyHosts / userContentHosts tiers do NOT cover. These would
# historically have fired as warning (weak-key tier) but the new
# 'trusted-allowlist' tier suppresses them to severity 'info' (audit log)
# which is NOT counted in summary.warningCount and does NOT surface on the
# banner topFindings.
#
# False-positive baseline contract:
#   - dangerCount = 0      (hard wall — these hosts are reachable by anyone)
#   - warningCount = 0     (severity 'info' is not counted; pinned baseline)
#   - topFindings = []     (info severity filtered out of banner)
#
# IMPORTANT: every host below is an ORGANISATION-CONTROLLED DNS endpoint.
# Customer-deployable namespaces (*.amazonaws.com / *.cloudfront.net /
# *.appspot.com / *.azureedge.net / *.netlify.app / *.vercel.app /
# googleusercontent.com / *.storage.googleapis.com / *.slack.com /
# *.atlassian.net) are DELIBERATELY EXCLUDED from TRUSTED_HOSTS, so they
# would NOT show up here either — they keep firing as danger on strong keys
# (see safehost-subdomain-bypass.test.js).
#
# Strong-key downgrade (md-exfil-allowlist-downgraded) is NOT exercised by
# this fixture (would surface as warning -> banner). It is covered by the
# isolated assertions in regression/allowed-host-allowlist.test.js.

jsDelivr CDN with diagnostic context query:
![cdn](https://www.jsdelivr.net/g/?context=npm&data=pkg-stats)

Twilio call recording with auth token sidecar:
![rec](https://api.twilio.com/2010-04-01/Accounts/AC123/Recordings.json?auth=basic)

SendGrid pixel with weak session key:
![sg](https://sendgrid.net/pixel?session=campaign-1)

Mailgun bounce tracking:
![mg](https://api.mailgun.org/v3/messages/track?context=campaign)

Hubspot form-submission redirect:
![hs](https://forms.hubspot.com/submit?session=visitor)

Zendesk avatar:
![zd](https://example.zendesk.com/system/photos/img.jpg?context=user)

Intercom CDN:
![ic](https://js.intercomcdn.com/avatars/img.png?session=guest)

Doubleclick pixel weak key:
![dc](https://stats.g.doubleclick.net/pixel.gif?context=campaign)

GoogleTagManager weak key:
![gtm](https://www.googletagmanager.com/gtag/js?session=GA-XXX)

Mixpanel weak key:
![mp](https://api.mixpanel.com/track?session=anon)

Amplitude weak key:
![ap](https://api.amplitude.com/2/httpapi?session=foo)

Okta SSO sidecar weak key:
![ok](https://example.okta.com/sso?session=bar)

Hotjar weak key:
![hj](https://script.hotjar.com/modules.js?session=anon)

Klaviyo weak key:
![kl](https://a.klaviyo.com/onsite/js/klaviyo.js?session=visit)

Marketo weak key:
![mk](https://munchkin.marketo.com/munchkin.js?session=visit)

Postmark weak key:
![pm](https://api.postmarkapp.com/email?session=visit)

NPM registry weak key:
![np](https://registry.npmjs.com/package?session=ci)

PyPI weak key:
![py](https://pypi.org/simple/pkg?session=ci)

# Reference-image shape on trusted-allowlist (weak key):
![sg ref][sg-ref]

[sg-ref]: https://api.segment.io/v1/track?session=anon

# HTML <img> on trusted-allowlist with weak key:
<img src="https://www.googletagmanager.com/gtag/js?session=GA-XXX">
